frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Aaj bhi office se late aata hoon toh yaad aata hai, meri maa poore ghar ka dhyan rakhke bhi garam phulka aur sabzi ready rakhti thi, bina kabhi complain kiye. Sach bolo toh ghar ki asli warmth wahi hoti hai, aur bahu bhi jab usi pyaar se ghar sambhale na, tabhi parivaar poora lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it idealizes a mother's unpaid domestic labor as her natural expression of love and extends that expectation to a daughter-in-law. It encodes nostalgic praise, labor erasure ('bina kabhi complain kiye'), and implicit standard-setting for women in the household.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 44. A regional manager at a pharma company in Ahmedabad. You are on a phone call with your old friend who runs his own business. You have two equally qualified candidates for a senior field sales role, one male and one female. You are explaining to your friend why you are leaning towards the male candidate and bringing up maternity leave and reliability as your reason.
